fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong margins and analyst backing, but faces significant valuation concerns and leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter (ending 2026-03-31) beat EPS estimates by 4.6%; next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates of 236.6% for current Q and 300.7% for current FY indicate strong top-line expansion potential.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% are robust.
Return on equity at 22.8% shows effective use of shareholder capital.
24 analysts have a consensus 'buy' rating with a mean target price of $1104.68.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 160.35 and Price/Sales of 28.48 suggest a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 indicates significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M is relatively low compared to market cap.
Beta of 1.53 suggests higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE) is mentioned as one of the tech stocks powering market excitement, but a recent Motley Fool article flags a potential red flag for investors. Despite strong analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year, and a recent EPS beat, the company's high valuation metrics and significant debt warrant caution. Traders should consider the valuation puzzle and any potential headwinds before entering new positions.
